prs.title: Fix Back to Editor hover state gap and icon visibility #3733
prs.body: ### Issue: Fixes #3733 The navigation header had a visual bug where the hover state of the “Back to Editor” button would expand into the gap between it and the p5* logo, making the two elements appear merged. Additionally, the left-arrow icon lacked proper contrast on hover, making it difficult to see against the hover background. This PR corrects the hover bounding box and ensures icon visibility. ### Demo: ### Changes: - **Refined Hover Boundary**: Removed the `padding-left` from the list item container (`.nav__item--no-icon`) and moved it inside the link element (`.nav__back-link`). This ensures the hover background highlight is scoped strictly to the button area. - **Fixed Icon Visibility**: Added a specific hover rule for the back-arrow icon to use `button-hover-color`, preventing it from "disappearing" against the hover background. - **Layout Consistency**: Set the link element to fill the container height (`height: 100%`) and used flex centering to ensure the clickable area is vertically consistent with other nav items. ### I have verified that this pull request: * [x] has no linting errors (`npm run lint`) * [x] has no test errors (`npm run test`) * [x] has no typecheck errors (`npm run typecheck`) * [x] is from a uniquely-named feature branch (`fix-back-to-editor-hover`) and is up to date with the `develop` branch. * [x] is descriptively named and links to an issue number. * [x] meets the standards outlined in the [accessibility guidelines](https://github.com/processing/p5.js-web-editor/blob/develop/contributor_docs/accessibility.md)

fix: favicon not visible for all contributors
prs.body: Fixes Issue: #640 This PR resolves the issue where the favicon was not appearing on contributor-related pages (`username/page.tsx` and `people/page.tsx`). --- **Changes Made** 1. Added the following to both username/page.tsx and people/page.tsx to ensure the correct favicon loads: ``` icons: { icon: config.meta.favicon_url, }, ``` 2. Removed the default favicon.ico from the Next.js public directory to prevent conflicts with the custom favicon. --- **Result** 1. Favicon now loads properly on all contributor profile pages. 2. Ensures consistent favicon behavior across dynamic routes.

prs.title: feat: add "Close library" to File menu
prs.body: Closes #14381 This PR adds three new library-management actions to the File menu: - **Close library** – closes the currently active library. - **Close others** – closes all libraries except the currently active one. - **Close all** – closes all open libraries. Previously, these actions were only available in the library tab’s context menu, making them harder to discover. Adding them to the File menu improves usability, aligns with common desktop application UX patterns, and provides faster access to library management operations. ### Steps to test 1. Start JabRef. 2. Open multiple libraries (e.g., Library A, Library B, Library C). 3. Go to **File → Close library**: - Only the active library should close. 4. Go to **File → Close others**: - The active library remains open. - All other libraries close. 5. Go to **File → Close all**: - All open libraries close. - JabRef returns to the empty state with no libraries open. ### Mandatory checks - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I license it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [x] I manually tested my changes in running JabRef (always required) - [/] I added JUnit tests for changes (if applicable) - [x] I added screenshots in the PR description (if change is visible to the user) - [/] I described the change in `CHANGELOG.md` in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [x] I checked the [user documentation](https://docs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I created an issue at or, even better, I submitted a pull request updating file(s) in .

prs.title: docs: update README with Flutter setup instructions
prs.body:
## Summary

This pull request updates the `README.md` to include accurate and up-to-date instructions for setting up the **Flutter development environment** for the PSLab application.  
The previous documentation referenced the old native Android setup, which is no longer applicable after the migration to Flutter.

---

## What's Included

- Added a complete Flutter setup guide (macOS, Linux, Windows)
- Documented required Flutter, Dart, and Java versions
- Updated Android development setup (Android Studio + CLI tools)
- Added iOS/macOS setup steps (Xcode, CocoaPods)
- Added Linux-specific instructions for PSLab USB device access
- Listed required runtime permissions and hardware features
- Added steps for running, building, and testing the Flutter app
- Added troubleshooting guidance for common build/setup issues

---

## Why This Change Is Needed

The project has transitioned to Flutter, but the README still referenced the native Android build workflow.  
This update ensures contributors can correctly set up the project, reducing confusion and improving onboarding.

---

## Issue Reference

Fixes #2959 — *Update README with instructions on setting up Flutter development environment*

## Summary by Sourcery

Update README with comprehensive Flutter development environment setup instructions replacing the outdated native Android guide

Documentation:
- Revise README to reflect migration to a Flutter cross-platform application supporting Android, iOS, macOS, Linux, Windows, and web
- Specify required Flutter, Dart, and Java versions for project compatibility
- Provide step-by-step installation and verification for Flutter SDK on macOS, Linux, and Windows
- Document Android development setup via Android Studio and command-line tools with SDK and license steps
- Add iOS/macOS setup instructions including Xcode and CocoaPods installation
- Include Linux-specific udev rules and group permissions for PSLab USB device access
- Outline project setup (clone, flutter pub get, platform-specific dependency installation)
- List required runtime permissions and key Flutter dependencies for hardware communication and sensors
- Detail commands for running, building (APK, appbundle, iOS, Linux), testing, and common troubleshooting scenarios

prs.title: docs: add guidelines for when presets should use removeTags
prs.body:
### Description, Motivation & Context

This PR adds guidelines explaining when presets should use `removeTags`. The goal is to prevent outdated or conflicting tags from remaining on features after editing, improve consistency across presets, and ensure deprecated or incompatible tags are cleaned up automatically.

Fixes #1800

### Related issues

_No directly related issues. This is a documentation improvement._

### Links and data

**Relevant OSM Wiki links:**
- https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Presets

**Relevant tag usage stats:**
> `remove=yes` is used in approximately 12,000 objects globally (Taginfo).  
> This shows it is actively used and proper guidelines help prevent misuse.

### Checklist and Test-Documentation Template

<details><summary>Read on to get your PR merged faster…</summary>

Follow these steps to test your PR yourself and make it a lot easier and faster for maintainers to check and approve it.

**This is how it works:**
1. After you submit your PR, the system will create a preview and comment on your PR:
   > 🍱 Your pull request preview is ready.
   If this is your first contribution to this project, the preview will not happen right away but requires a click from one of the project members. We will do this ASAP.

2. Once the preview is ready, use it to test your changes.

3. Now copy the snippet below into a new comment and fill out the blanks.

4. Now your PR is ready to be reviewed.


```
## Test-Documentation

### Preview links & Sidebar Screenshots

<!-- Use the preview to find examples, select the feature in question and **copy this link here**.
     Find examples of nodes/areas. Find examples with a lot of tags or very few tags. – Whatever helps to test this thoroughly.
     Add relevant **screenshots** of the sidebar of those examples. -->

<!-- FYI: What we will check:
     - Is the [icon](https://github.com/ideditor/schema-builder/blob/main/ICONS.md) well chosen.
     - Are the fields well-structured and have good labels.
     - Do the dropdowns (etc.) work well and show helpful data. -->

### Search

<!-- **Test the search** of your preset and share relevant **screenshots** here.
     - Test the preset name as search terms.
     - Also test the preset terms and aliases as search terms (if present). -->

### Info-`i`

<!-- **Test the info-i** for your fields and preset and share relevant **screenshots** here.
     The info needs to help mappers understand the preset and when to use it.
     [Learn more…](https://github.com/openstreetmap/id-tagging-schema/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#info-i)
 -->

### Wording

- [ ] American English
- [ ] `name`, `aliases` (if present) use Title Case
- [ ] `terms` (if present) use lower case, sorted A-Z
<!-- Learn more in https://github.com/openstreetmap/id-tagging-schema/blob/main/GUIDELINES.md#2-design-the-preset -->
```

</details>

issues.title: BUG: Unable to start the project for development
issues.body: Hello, whenever I follow the steps given in the README.md to initialise set up the dev environment, it doesn't seem to work. ### Backend The backend, gives me an error reporting `DebateWebsocketHandler` as undefined. ### Frontend The frontend, gives me an error about double declaration of a variable `toggleCamera` in `frontend/src/Pages/TeamDebateRoom.tsx`. Upon opening the file, I'm noticing a lot of other syntax errors, that are being caught by my linter.
